payload.title: Hub watchdog + runtime repair ("self-healing hub")
payload.description:
Make the federated hub self-healing so the UI never goes blank for surviving sessions when the hub-owning process exits. Today every `scope ui/serve/mcp` registers with whichever process won the port bind. When that process dies, other registered workspaces still serve stdio MCP but lose UI access until a human reruns a command.

## Design

Every long-lived scope process (the stdio MCP in `scope mcp`, the idling-on-attach process in `scope ui`/`scope serve`) starts a watchdog that:

1. Polls the hub's `/api/meta` every ~10s.
2. If three consecutive probes fail, calls `ensureHub({ scopeDir, label })` again. The existing race-safe logic means one of the surviving processes wins the bind and the rest re-attach.
3. On successful re-bind, the new hub rehydrates `~/.scope-hub/workspaces.json` (already implemented), so the workspace switcher is repopulated automatically.

## UI side

The refresh button in the web UI should also trigger the same logic from the client side: re-fetch `/api/meta`, and if it fails, surface "Hub is down — waiting for re-election" while the runtime repair on the server side promotes a replacement. When `/events` SSE reconnects, the UI seamlessly resumes.

## Why this is high priority

This is the only failure mode that actually bites in normal multi-session use — and it's fixable without adding any install ceremony. After [[real-hub-daemon-launchd-systemd]], this watchdog logic becomes obsolete (or a fallback), but until then it closes the gap.
